title: Why Word Choice Changes the Output
concept: Because generation is directly steered by embeddings of specific words, small prompt wording changes can meaningfully shift the generated image. Boss explain ends with: 'Next up: Stable Diffusion Architecture.'

exercises.prompt: If you asked an artist to draw 'a happy dog' vs 'a joyful puppy,' would the pictures look exactly the same?

options.text: No, they'd likely be a bit different

exercises.explain: Different words carry different shades of meaning, so even a small wording change can lead to a different picture — same idea applies to AI image generators!

cards.title: One Word, New World
cards.body: Swap 'castle' for 'palace' in a prompt, and the AI image can completely transform — different towers, colors, even mood! Why does a tiny word swap create such a big visual change? Let's find out.

cards.title: Word Choice Steers Generation
cards.body: AI art models turn each word into a special number code called an embedding. These embeddings guide the whole image-making process. Since different words have different embeddings, even small wording changes can meaningfully shift what the AI creates.

cards.title: A Compass Bearing
cards.body: Think of each word as a compass direction that nudges the image-making process. Pointing 1 degree off course might not seem like much at first, but over a long journey, it lands you somewhere totally different. Small word shifts nudge the 'direction' of the whole image.

cards.title: Dragon vs Lizard
cards.body: Prompt A: 'a fierce dragon guarding treasure.' Prompt B: 'a fierce lizard guarding treasure.' Even though 'dragon' and 'lizard' seem similar, their embeddings differ enough that the AI produces very different creatures — one mythical and huge, one small and reptilian.

cards.title: Myth: Synonyms Are Interchangeable
cards.body: Myth: Using a 'similar' word always gives basically the same image. Truth: Words that seem like synonyms (like 'cottage' vs 'shack') can have different embeddings, nudging color, mood, and detail in noticeably different directions.

cards.title: Small Edit, Big Difference
cards.body: Compare how one changed word shifts the whole image's feel.

left.text: Weathered stone towers, warm orange sky, crumbling walls

right.text: Elegant gold-trimmed structure, grand pillars, softer glow

exercises.prompt: Why can changing just one word in a prompt change the whole generated image?

options.text: Because the AI gets confused and guesses randomly
Because each word has its own embedding that helps steer the image
Because longer prompts always look better
Because the AI ignores all words except the first one

exercises.explain: Every word is converted into an embedding, a numeric code that guides the generation process, so different words can steer the output differently.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Words that seem like synonyms always produce identical AI-generated images.
exercises.explain: False! Even similar-sounding words can have different embeddings, which can shift the details, mood, or style of the generated image.

exercises.sentence: Each word in a prompt is turned into an ___ that helps steer what the AI generates.
exercises.explain: The embedding is the numeric code representing a word's meaning, and it's what guides the image generation process.

exercises.prompt: A student wants their AI-generated picture of a 'small cozy hut' to look more grand and elegant. What should they try?

options.text: Keep the exact same prompt and hope it changes
Change 'small cozy hut' to something like 'grand elegant mansion'

exercises.explain: Swapping in words with embeddings closer to 'grand' and 'elegant' will steer the AI toward a different, fancier-looking image.

exercises.prompt: Why do small changes in prompt wording lead to meaningfully different AI-generated images?

options.text: Because generation is directly steered by embeddings of specific words, and different words have different embeddings
Because the AI randomly picks a new image every time regardless of the prompt
Because only the number of words matters, not their meaning
Because AI models ignore adjectives completely

exercises.explain: Since every word becomes an embedding that steers the image generation process, even a small wording change can nudge the output in a noticeably different direction. Next up: Stable Diffusion Architecture.
